blocks.body: No, none, neither, either are quantifiers expressing absence or choice between two options. No and none mean "none", and neither and either are used when talking about two objects.

{ "title": "10. Short summary", "items": [ "No comes before a noun: no money, no students.", "None works alone or with of: none came, none of them.", "Neither is negative and refers to two options: neither option, neither of them.", "Either usually means any one of two: either option, either of them.", "Neither... nor... connects two negative alternatives.", "Either... or... connects two possible alternatives.", "Use neither/either for two options. For three or more, use none or another quantifier.", "Do not add not after neither; the negative meaning is already there." ] }
